reasoning: The French weather station at Saint-Pierre (WMO 71805) is located in Saint Pierre and Miquelon, which has a sub-polar marine climate. Temperatures drop significantly and consistently from October (average mean ~8°C) through January (average mean ~-3°C). October 26 typically sees mean daily temperatures around 6-7°C. Because winter dates will have a significantly lower mean temperature, the probability of subsequent dates (especially deep into November, December, or January) being warmer than October 26 relies on weather anomalies—such as an unusually cold late October day paired with an abnormally warm winter day. Given typical standard deviations in daily weather, the likelihood of a mid-winter day exceeding late October temperatures is low, estimated at around 10-15% depending on the exact resolution dates (which are presumed to be in the following months up to the January 2026 close date).
